steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank must complete the contrast: we expect a helpful person to be frequently helpful, not ______ helpful. The missing word names a stronger standard than 'frequently' — something meaning always or without exception — because that is the overly strict standard the lay view does not require.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — unfailingly: Means without ever failing — i.e., always, 100% of the time. Substituting: 'we expect a helpful person to be frequently helpful, not unfailingly helpful.' That contrast works perfectly; the lay view sets a realistic bar (frequently), not an absolute one (unfailingly). ✓
  • Choice B — self-servingly: Means acting for personal gain. Substituting gives 'not self-servingly helpful,' which changes the meaning to motivation rather than frequency. Does not fit the contrast with 'frequently.' ✗
  • Choice C — grudgingly: Means reluctantly. Substituting gives 'not grudgingly helpful,' which shifts to attitude, not frequency. Does not complete the contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — sporadically: Means occasionally or irregularly — actually less frequent than 'frequently,' not more. The contrast would be backwards. ✗
